name: gallery-html-patterns
description: Static HTML gallery architecture patterns — single-file bundled viewer with vanilla JavaScript, responsive CSS grid, dark mode theming, metadata-driven rendering, and accessibility for the archiver's gallery output
---

# Gallery HTML Patterns

Architecture and implementation patterns for chatgpt-library-archiver's static HTML gallery — a single bundled file with no external dependencies.

**When to use this skill:**
- Modifying the gallery viewer (`gallery_index.html`)
- Adding gallery features (filtering, sorting, new views)
- Improving accessibility or responsive behavior
- Fixing rendering issues across browsers
- Understanding the gallery's metadata-driven architecture

## Architecture Overview

The gallery is a **single HTML file** (`gallery_index.html`) bundled with the Python package and copied to the gallery root during generation. It reads `metadata.json` at runtime to render the image collection.

```
gallery/
├── index.html           ← copied from gallery_index.html
├── metadata.json        ← generated by Python, read by JS
├── images/              ← full-size images
└── thumbs/
    ├── small/           ← 150×150
    ├── medium/          ← 250×250
    └── large/           ← 400×400
```

### Data Flow

1. Browser loads `index.html`
2. JavaScript fetches `metadata.json` via `fetch()`
3. Items are parsed, filtered, sorted, and paginated
4. DOM is built from metadata using `createElement` / `innerHTML`
5. Thumbnail `<img>` tags point to `thumbs/<size>/<filename>`
6. Full-size links point to `images/<filename>`

## CSS Architecture

### Theming with Custom Properties

```css
:root {
  --bg: #fff;
  --text: #000;
  --meta-color: #444;
  --control-bg: #ddd;
}
body.dark {
  --bg: #111;
  --text: #eee;
  --meta-color: #ccc;
  --control-bg: #555;
}
```

All colors reference CSS custom properties so dark mode works by toggling the `dark` class on `<body>`.

### Responsive Grid

```css
.gallery-grid {
  display: grid;
  gap: var(--gap);
  grid-template-columns: repeat(auto-fill, minmax(var(--thumb-size, 200px), 1fr));
  grid-auto-rows: var(--thumb-size, 200px);
}
```

Size classes control the grid cell size:
- `.gallery-small` → 150px
- `.gallery-medium` → 250px
- `.gallery-large` → 400px
- `.gallery-full` → full-width list view

### Image Cards

```css
.image-card {
  position: relative;
  border-radius: 8px;
  overflow: hidden;
  height: 100%;
}
.image-card img {
  max-width: 100%;
  max-height: 100%;
  object-fit: contain;
  object-position: center;
}
```

## JavaScript Patterns

### No Frameworks

All JavaScript is vanilla ES6+. No jQuery, no React, no build step.

### Metadata Loading

```javascript
async function loadMetadata() {
  const response = await fetch('metadata.json');
  const items = await response.json();
  return items;
}
```

### Filtering

The gallery supports filtering by title search, date range, and tags. Filters are combined with AND logic (all filters must match).

### Pagination

Client-side pagination renders a fixed number of items per page. Navigation controls allow page traversal.

### Lightbox

Click on an image card to open a full-size lightbox overlay. Navigate with arrow keys or swipe. Press Escape to close.

## Accessibility Checklist

When modifying the gallery:

- [ ] All images have `alt` text (use `title` or `filename`)
- [ ] Interactive controls have visible focus indicators
- [ ] Controls have `aria-label` or visible text labels
- [ ] Lightbox traps focus while open
- [ ] Lightbox responds to Escape key
- [ ] Color contrast meets WCAG 2.1 AA (4.5:1 for text)
- [ ] Gallery works with keyboard-only navigation (Tab, Enter, Arrows)
- [ ] Skip-to-content link for keyboard users
- [ ] Semantic HTML: `<article>`, `<nav>`, `<figure>`, `<figcaption>`

## Performance Guidelines

- **Thumbnail selection**: Grid view loads `small` thumbnails; lightbox loads `large`
- **Lazy loading**: Use `loading="lazy"` on `<img>` elements below the fold
- **Debounce filters**: Filter/search input callbacks should be debounced (200-300ms)
- **Batch DOM updates**: Build HTML strings or use `DocumentFragment`, then insert once
- **Minimize reflows**: Avoid reading layout properties (offsetHeight) between writes

## Adding New Features

When adding gallery features:

1. **HTML**: Add controls to the toolbar section at the top
2. **CSS**: Use custom properties for new colors; ensure dark mode compatibility
3. **JavaScript**: Keep functions small and focused; attach events via `addEventListener`
4. **Test**: Verify with both light and dark themes, multiple screen sizes, and keyboard navigation
5. **Keep it one file**: Everything stays in `gallery_index.html`
